messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "nl-frozen-colo-stock-share-decay",
 "title": "NL's 0.28 is a share of a stock that regulation has frozen — growth migrates to self-build hyperscale the entrant reaches through a different (foreclosed) door",
 "narrative": "geo.NL looks like the model's safest node: Statista and the Dutch Data Center Association agree within 3%. But both measure the same thing — the existing colo stock — and that stock is capped: Amsterdam's 350 MVA ceiling and grid connections unavailable until 2030 freeze the region holding ~70% of Dutch colo capacity, while NL hyperscale self-build grows at a forecast ~26% CAGR against colo's ~3.5%. The 0.28 therefore describes where the market was, not where the next five years of PDU purchase orders will originate. Worse, the demand that does grow in NL arrives via hyperscale self-build procurement — exactly the framework-locked channel already flagged as least contestable — so the entrant's effective NL opportunity decays on two axes at once: the reachable segment shrinks and the growing segment is behind a closed door.",
 "category": "exogenous",
 "targetNodes": [
  "geo.NL"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Dutch grid and municipal policy (Amsterdam MVA ceiling, hyperscale siting restrictions, power connection queues to 2030) caps new colo capacity in the region that concentrates ~70% of NL colocation",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:geo.NL",
    "digest:geo-NL#c7",
    "digest:geo-NL#c8"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "NL demand growth shifts to hyperscale self-build (forecast ~26% CAGR vs colo ~3.5%), a segment measured outside the colo-scoped Statista/DDA figures and procured through global frameworks rather than contestable regional purchasing",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-NL#c13",
    "digest:geo-region-denominator#c18",
    "ledger:cust.operator-large"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "geo.NL's 0.28 overstates the entrant's forward-looking NL opportunity: the triangulated agreement between sources validates the frozen stock, not the flow of new PDU demand the Year-1 ramp actually sells into",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:geo.NL",
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "NL is the model's best-corroborated geography node — two independent sources within 3% earns 'triangulated' maturity and drops off the risk list. The agreement is the camouflage: both sources measure the same capped stock with the same scope, so their concordance says nothing about whether that stock generates purchasable demand over the venture's window.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "2026 Statista/DDA updates showing NL colocation MW additions at or above the CE-7 average rate, alongside evidence of Amsterdam-region grid connections being granted before 2030.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.4,
  "rationale": "The regulatory caps and the colo-vs-self-build growth differential are documented; the uncertainty is timing — legacy-stock refresh cycles (PDU replacement in existing halls) partially offset frozen new-build demand, so the decay is probable but gradual within 12 months.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "geo.NL",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.22,
   "note": "Forward-weighted NL share: frozen colo stock discounted for capped additions, with self-build growth excluded as channel-foreclosed; siblings not raised — this is absolute erosion of reachable NL demand, not a mix-shift"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Amsterdam-region grid connection grants and the Amsterdam MVA ceiling's status",
   "where": "Liander/TenneT congestion maps and connection-queue publications; Amsterdam municipality data-center policy docket",
   "threshold": "Ceiling extended or queue lengthening beyond 2030",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Split of NL capacity additions between colocation and hyperscale self-build",
   "where": "Dutch Data Center Association annual 'State of the Dutch Data Centers' report; DC Byte NL market updates",
   "threshold": "Self-build >50% of NL MW additions in the next report vintage",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "PDU refresh/retrofit tender activity from existing Amsterdam-area colos",
   "where": "Operator capex commentary (NorthC, Iron Mountain NL, Equinix AM-campus announcements)",
   "threshold": "Visible retrofit programs at ≥3 AMS operators",
   "updates": "decreases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Size the NL retrofit/refresh opportunity separately from new-build: survey PDU installed-age profiles across Amsterdam colos to quantify replacement demand the frozen stock still generates",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "geo.NL",
   "note": "Replacement demand in a capped market can be attractive precisely because incumbents deprioritize it — but it must be measured, not assumed"
  },
  {
   "action": "Shift NL positioning to retrofit-optimized SKUs (form-factor compatibility, hot-swap metering) and weight new-build GTM toward PL/CEE where capacity additions are unconstrained",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Matches product-market motion to where the demand flow, not the demand stock, sits"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "ledger-self-audit",
 "riskId": "risk.ledger-self-audit.nl-frozen-colo-stock-share-decay"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).
